**Test and Characterization of Laser Diodes:
Determination of Principal Parameters
By Kamran S. Mobarhan, Ph.D.**
This hand book is around determining the principle parameters to characterize and optimize laser to its efficient point.
[Notes](https://web.goodnotes.com/s/RIr0x17NwiZTeFIQFdGrsT)

**Report:first week status**

- After reading through the above mentioned paper, it made sense about the way of characterizing the diode, but i was caught up
into thinking about difference we will have in characterization of External cavity diode, from just a simple diode. Although i havent figured 
out how to totally solve my problem, but there were few characterizations which seem of interest and which can be directly translated for any diode.
- Few mentionable parameters that were mentioned in paper that i would like to look into and try to have numerical simulation of are:
    - Output Light vs. Input Current Curve
    and Threshold Current:
    - External Differential Quantum Efficiency
    - Internal Quantum Efficiency (I am not sure of this, as it is dependent on geometery of the diode)
    - Characteristic Temperature
    - Spectrum and Peak Wavelength of Emission
    - Center Wavelength Changes with Temperature
    - Mode Hopping
    
I am interesting in looking into the above parameter and how their dynamics work with temperature and change of current.
If it is to be asked from me at this moment what is goal of looking into these parameter, and what information am i expecting
to gain from it? my answer right now would be i am not sure, and that is why i would like to make this as a goal to understand 
the information this will give me and present it in coherent manner.

**Second Week Goal**
- Try to have numerical model of these parameter and play around with the inputs and observe dynamics of output and understand the behaviour,
and in mean while take data of inputs from real experimental setup and observe if our understanding of dynamics make sense or not, and what 
pieces of puzzle are we missing.


**Threshold Current**
: $I_{th}$ threshold point of input current to diode at which it begins to lase.
    low threshold current means more efficient diode.


**P-I Curve**
: $frac{\Delta P}{\Delta T}$ It shows change in output power over change in input current. It is also refered as L-I curve, Where L refers to luminosity, and I to current.
    P is measure of luminosity in watt, I is measure of current

**Luminosity**
:Typically it refers to typical optical output power produced by given amount of input current.

**External Differential Quantum Efficiency**
: $\eta_d$ indicates the ability of laser device to convert input current(injected electron hole pair) to photon emitted from the device(output light)
    $\eta_d=frac{\Delta P/Delta I}{hc/q \lambda}$
    h is planks constant, \lambda is wavelength, c is speed of light, q is charge of electron.
    When talking about External Differential Quantum Efficiency one should always note if diode is one mirror facet or two mirror facet.

**Perfect Hypothetical Device**
: The device that works with 100% efficiency without wasting energy in any unrequired form.

**Current Density**
: $J$ is quantity which is ratio of current to area(area is consist of length of diode and width of ridge(we need to have better defination of it)). Density is more useful quantity when comparing different diodes.

**Internal Quantum Efficiency**
: $\eta_i$ It is measure of efficiency of laser diode in convertng electron hole pairs(input current) into photons(light) within laser diode structure.
    $\eta_i$ is independet of geometrical properties of laser device, which makes it proper parameter for comparision of material quality of various laser devices.
It is determined by plotting inverse external differential quantum efficiency(y axis) vs cavity length(x axis)

Once we have $\eta_d$ and $\eta_i$ we can calculate ratio $\frac{eta_d}{\eta_i}$ which is photon generated to photon emitted ratio.

**Internal Loss of Laser Diode**
: Parameter that corresponds to loss of optical wave(need to know more about it)

**Characteristic Temperature**
$T_o$ It is measure of temperature sensitivity of Laser Diode. Higher values of T_o imply that thr threshold current density and external differential quantum efficiency of laser diode increases
 rapidly with increasing temperatures(meaning laser diode being more thermally stable.)
    to measure characteristic temperature we need to measure P-I curve at different temperatures.

**Central Wavelength**

**Mode Hopping**
